query: I need to implement the CartSummary read model in src/Store/Cart/Queries/CartSummary.hs. The domain-modeling step already stubbed the types and the Projection spec is failing with a panic. I need to fill in the real combine logic: count items from the CartEntity array, set isEmpty = count == 0, and return Update with the denormalised CartSummary record. Also need canAccess and canView before the deriveQuery splice.

Write Queries/StockLevel.hs for the Stock context. It should derive from StockEntity, expose available and reserved fields, use publicAccess/publicView for auth, and have the QueryOf instance with a simple Update in combine — no computed fields, just direct projection from the entity. Run neo build at the end to confirm deriveQuery spliced cleanly.

green phase for the OrderSummary feature — projection spec is red on the panic stub. I need the real QueryOf instance for OrderEntity -> OrderSummary, with combine that sets totalAmount by summing order.lineItems, returns Update when items are present, NoOp when the entity has no lines yet. Also wire canAccess = authenticatedAccess and canView = ownerOnly (.ownerId) before the deriveQuery macro.

neo build is blowing up: ERROR: Missing 'canAccess' function on the deriveQuery splice in Queries/BookLoan.hs. I have the record and the QueryOf instance but forgot the auth functions. Add both canAccess (authenticatedAccess) and canView (ownerOnly (.borrowerId)) above the deriveQuery ''BookLoan [''LoanEntity] line so the TH macro can resolve them.

multi-entity read model: MemberLoanSummary needs to aggregate from both MemberEntity and LoanEntity. MemberEntity provides the name and creates the instance (combine returns Update with activeLoanCount preserved from maybeExisting), LoanEntity increments activeLoanCount but returns NoOp when maybeExisting is Nothing so it doesn't create phantom instances. Two QueryOf instances, deriveQuery ''MemberLoanSummary [''MemberEntity, ''LoanEntity]. How do I write this file?

my Projection spec for InventorySummary keeps failing because combine returns Update even when the entity has been deleted — i need it to return Delete when stock.isArchived is True and Update otherwise. also the canView is missing the per-instance check, right now its just publicView but it should restrict to ownerOnly. can u fix src/Warehouse/Inventory/Queries/InventorySummary.hs

I'm adding a new read model for the Library domain: BorrowingSummary aggregates a MemberEntity and returns a denormalised view with memberName, borrowedCount, overdueCount. The event-model.json already has a verified BorrowingSummaryQueried node with the field list. I need the full Queries/BorrowingSummary.hs — record, Json/ToSchema instances, canAccess, canView, deriveQuery, and the QueryOf instance with combine logic that computes overdue from loan.dueDate compared to the current snapshot date stored on the entity.

combine body question: for my CartSummary query, if the cart entity has status == Abandoned I want to emit Delete so the read model is removed from the store. Otherwise emit Update with the usual denormalised fields. The maybeExisting arg is not needed for this one. Is there anything special about returning Delete vs Update in NeoHaskell's QueryOf — does it affect how deriveQuery registers the subscriber?

I need to write the Projection spec for CartSummary — set up a list of CartItemAdded events, replay them through the entity updater, then assert that combine produces an Update with the correct itemCount. The spec should cover the NoOp branch too (empty entity). Where should the file go and what imports does a Projection spec need in NeoHaskell?

Implement the AddToCart command handler in src/Store/Cart/Commands/AddToCart.hs. I need the decide function that checks CartEntity state and emits CartItemAdded, and the canExecute auth check using RequestContext. The command already exists in event-model.json — I just need the Green-phase implementation.

CartEntity needs to track an items array of CartItem records and an ownerId field, and the update function should handle CartItemAdded by appending to items and CartCleared by resetting the array. domain-modeling already stubbed the types with panic — I need the real update logic so the entity is correct before the query layer reads from it.

The StockLevel query is implemented and tested but I still need to register it with the feature router. Walk me through adding withQuery @StockLevel to the feature wiring in Wire.hs so the HTTP endpoint is live and the hurl e2e test can hit it.

I'm starting the CartSummary feature from scratch and need to stub the types: CartSummary record with cartSummaryId, ownerId, itemCount fields, and a panic-body QueryOf instance so the module compiles but tests stay red. What's the correct DOMAIN-phase setup before the green phase?

My event-model.json has a new CartSummaryQueried node but I'm not sure all the field names are consistent with CartEntity and the CartItemAdded event. Can you verify the event model graph — check that queryId references the right entity key, the output fields exist on the entity, and the node is connected correctly before I start implementation?

The CartEntity record is missing a reservedItems field that the CartSummary query will need for its combine logic. I need to expand the entity: add the field to the record, update the CartItemReserved event handler in the update function, make sure the existing Projection specs still pass after the expansion.

I need to call the external Stripe API from inside the checkout feature to verify payment status before fulfilling an order. The integration should handle HTTP errors, retry on 5xx, and map the Stripe PaymentIntent status to our domain OrderPaymentStatus type. Where does this live in a NeoHaskell feature and what does the module structure look like?
